questions.question: Explain the difference between distance and displacement with suitable examples.
questions.answer: Distance is the total path travelled by an object whereas displacement is the shortest distance between initial and final positions in a specific direction.

questions.question: Explain how motion depends on the reference point with examples.
questions.answer: Motion is relative to the reference point. An object may appear moving relative to one reference point but stationary relative to another.

questions.question: Explain uniform motion and non-uniform motion with examples and graphical representation.
questions.answer: Uniform motion occurs when equal distances are covered in equal intervals of time while non-uniform motion occurs when unequal distances are covered in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: Explain speed and velocity and compare them with examples.
questions.answer: Speed is the rate of distance travelled while velocity is the rate of displacement with direction.

questions.question: Explain acceleration and its types with examples.
questions.answer: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with time. Types include positive acceleration, negative acceleration and uniform acceleration.

questions.question: Explain the concept of average speed and instantaneous speed.
questions.answer: Average speed is total distance divided by total time while instantaneous speed is the speed at a particular instant.

questions.question: Explain the distance-time graph and its significance.
questions.answer: A distance-time graph shows how distance changes with time. Its slope represents speed.

questions.question: Explain the velocity-time graph and its applications.
questions.answer: Velocity-time graph shows the change of velocity with time. The slope represents acceleration and the area under the graph represents displacement.

questions.question: Explain the three equations of motion and their applications.
questions.answer: The equations of motion relate velocity, acceleration, time and displacement for uniformly accelerated motion.

questions.question: Explain the conditions under which equations of motion are applicable.
questions.answer: They are applicable only when acceleration is uniform and motion occurs in a straight line.

questions.question: Explain uniform circular motion with examples.
questions.answer: Uniform circular motion occurs when an object moves along a circular path with constant speed.

questions.question: Explain why circular motion is considered accelerated motion.
questions.answer: Although speed remains constant, the direction of velocity continuously changes which causes acceleration.

questions.question: Explain the importance of graphs in analyzing motion.
questions.answer: Graphs help determine speed, acceleration and displacement and make it easier to analyze motion patterns.

questions.question: Explain the SI units used in motion and their importance.
questions.answer: SI units standardize measurement of motion quantities such as metre for distance and metre per second for speed.

questions.question: Explain the role of speedometer and odometer in vehicles.
questions.answer: Speedometer measures the speed of vehicles while odometer records the distance travelled.

questions.question: Explain how acceleration affects velocity and motion.
questions.answer: Acceleration can increase speed, decrease speed or change the direction of velocity.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between displacement, velocity and acceleration.
questions.answer: These quantities are related through the equations of motion in uniformly accelerated motion.

questions.question: Explain why displacement can sometimes be zero while distance is not.
questions.answer: If an object returns to its starting position the displacement becomes zero while distance remains positive.

questions.question: Explain motion in terms of scalar and vector quantities.
questions.answer: Motion quantities such as distance and speed are scalars while displacement and velocity are vectors.

questions.question: Explain how motion is classified based on the path followed.
questions.answer: Motion can be classified as linear motion, circular motion and oscillatory motion depending on the path followed.

questions.question: Explain the motion of satellites around Earth.
questions.answer: Satellites revolve around Earth in circular paths due to gravitational force showing uniform circular motion.

questions.question: Explain how gravity affects motion of objects.
questions.answer: Gravity causes objects to accelerate toward Earth and is responsible for free fall.

questions.question: Explain the motion of Earth around the Sun.
questions.answer: Earth moves around the Sun in a nearly circular orbit showing uniform circular motion.

questions.question: Explain why studying motion is important in science.
questions.answer: Understanding motion helps in designing vehicles, studying planetary movement and solving engineering problems.

questions.question: Explain how equations of motion help solve real life problems.
questions.answer: Equations of motion help calculate velocity, distance and acceleration in situations such as vehicle movement or falling objects.
